schema_amendments.description: Array tracking derivation path for every constant or structural condition. principle_10_compliant true only when all entries anchored at live edges or IR axioms.
Boolean gate. Circularity detection: paths must terminate at (a) live edge from published AI layer or (b) IR rule applied to mathematical axiom.

paper_title: The Full Conservation Law: Epistemic Information Vanishes for All Measurement Functions

scope_boundary.IN: All measurement functions M satisfying condition (b) of T(P,O,M) — finite deterministic functions from observation classes to verdict spaces. Includes binary, n-ary, continuous, and probabilistic M.
OUT: Non-terminating M (fail condition (b), not well-formed predicates)
Stochastic M that are not finite deterministic (fail condition (b))
The UMP itself (proved in DRP-1; DRP-4 uses the conservation consequence, not the independence condition)
scope_boundary.scope_note: DRP-4 generalises the binary conservation law I(M(O);P)=0 from DRP-2 to all terminating M. The generalisation is structural: the I=0 mechanism depends only on O=f(P), not on M's output type.

inbound_edges.label: Binary epistemic conservation law: I(M(O);P) = 0 for binary M when O = f(P)
Convergence Principle: M-termination ↔ μ<0. All terminating M share the same convergence structure.

outbound_edges_register.label: Full Conservation Law: I(M(O);P) = 0 for all terminating M when O = f(P). Received as Type A axiom in DRP-5.

inference_rules.name: Received Axiom
inference_rules.form: A claim proved in a prior paper with LIVE status may be received as Type A without re-derivation.

inference_rules.name: Structural Generalisation
inference_rules.form: If a theorem proved for a restricted case X depends only on structural property S, and S holds for the general case Y ⊃ X, then the theorem holds for Y.

inference_rules.name: Composition Preservation
inference_rules.form: If f: A→B and g: B→C are both determined by the same upstream variable, then g∘f is determined by that variable. Determinism is preserved under composition.

inference_rules.name: Exhaustive Classification
inference_rules.form: If a domain D is partitioned into classes C1...Cn and a property P holds for each Ci, then P holds for all of D.

inference_rules.name: Vacuity Witness
inference_rules.form: A falsification predicate is non-vacuous (C6) iff a coherent observation exists that would trigger FALSIFIED.

claim_registry.label: Binary conservation law — received from DRP-2
claim_registry.statement: For any falsification predicate K = (P, O, M, B) where O = f(P) and M is binary (M: O → {FALSIFIED, NOT FALSIFIED}): I(M(O); P) = 0. The mutual information between the test outcome and the proposition is zero when the observation class is derived from the proposition.

claim_registry.label: Convergence Principle — received from DRP-3
claim_registry.statement: M-termination is epistemically equivalent to μ<0. Both are instances of the same abstract fixed-point convergence requirement. All terminating M functions share the same convergence structure regardless of output type.

claim_registry.label: M-class taxonomy
claim_registry.statement: The class of all measurement functions M satisfying condition (b) of T(P,O,M) (finite deterministic) is partitioned into four exhaustive sub-classes by output type: (i) binary M: O → {FALSIFIED, NOT FALSIFIED}; (ii) n-ary M: O → {v1, ..., vn} for finite n > 2; (iii) continuous M: O → ℝ (or a measurable subset); (iv) probabilistic M: O → [0,1] (interpreted as probability or confidence). All four sub-classes satisfy condition (b) when M terminates. No well-formed M exists outside this partition.

claim_registry.label: Generalised epistemic information
claim_registry.statement: For any M satisfying condition (b) of T(P,O,M), the epistemic information content I(M(O); P) is defined as: the reduction in uncertainty about P achieved by observing M(O), measured as the difference between prior uncertainty H(P) and posterior uncertainty H(P|M(O)). I(M(O); P) = H(P) − H(P|M(O)). This definition applies uniformly across all M-classes (C-DRP4.3) and reduces to Shannon mutual information when M is binary.

claim_registry.label: The full conservation law
claim_registry.statement: For any falsification predicate K = (P, O, M, B) where O = f(P) and M satisfies condition (b) of T(P,O,M): I(M(O); P) = 0. The epistemic information content of the test is zero regardless of M's output type. This generalises C-DRP4.1 (binary case) to all terminating M.

WHERE.n_counterexamples: integer, dimensionless. Count of falsification predicates K = (P, O, M, B) simultaneously satisfying: (a) O = f(P) — observation class derived from the proposition; (b) M satisfies condition (b) of T(P,O,M) — M is finite deterministic and terminates; (c) I(M(O); P) > 0 — the test outcome provides non-zero epistemic information about P, measured as reduction in prior uncertainty. A single instance of (a)+(b)+(c) suffices to falsify.

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: 1. Construct candidate predicate K with O = f(P) and non-binary M (binary case already covered by C-DRP4.1). 2. Verify M satisfies condition (b): finite, deterministic, terminates. 3. Compute h = M ∘ f. Verify h(P) is a deterministic function of P. 4. Compute I(h(P); P) using the generalised measure (C-DRP4.4). 5. If I > 0: n_counterexamples += 1. Output: n_counterexamples. Finite procedure.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: n_counterexamples = 0 → NOT FALSIFIED. n_counterexamples = 1 → FALSIFIED (universal claim; one counterexample suffices).
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-DRP4.5 · Type F · Full conservation law — central claim. Generalises DRP-2 C-DRP2.2 from binary to all M. Tests whether a non-binary M with O=f(P) can produce I > 0. Threshold: 0. UMP SATISFIED: O (candidate predicates with measured I) upstream of P (conservation law). M (computation of I) finite deterministic. C6 SATISFIED: counterexample system would produce FALSIFIED.

claim_registry.sample_falsification_observation: A continuous M computing a test statistic from O=f(P) that yields I > 0 — i.e., the test statistic provides information about P despite O being derived from P. Believed non-existent because h(P) = M(f(P)) is determined by P, so H(P|h(P)) = H(P) and I = 0.

claim_registry.label: Mechanism independence
claim_registry.statement: The I=0 result in DRP-2 §3 depends on exactly one structural property: O = f(P). It does not invoke any property specific to binary M. The cardinality, topology, or measure-theoretic structure of M's output space plays no role in the proof that I = 0.

WHERE.n_binary_steps: integer, dimensionless. Count of steps in the DRP-2 §3 proof of I(M(O); P) = 0 that invoke a property specific to binary M — i.e., steps whose justification requires |range(M)| = 2 or the structure {FALSIFIED, NOT FALSIFIED} as a necessary premise, and which cannot be re-derived for general M.

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: 1. Obtain DRP-2 v1.2 §3 proof text. 2. For each step: identify the justification. 3. Determine whether the justification requires |range(M)| = 2 or any binary-specific property. 4. If a step requires binary M and cannot be re-derived without it: n_binary_steps += 1. Output: n_binary_steps. Finite procedure (proof is a finite text).
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: n_binary_steps = 0 → NOT FALSIFIED. n_binary_steps = 1 → FALSIFIED (single binary-dependent step would block generalisation).
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-DRP4.6 · Type F · Mechanism independence. Tests whether DRP-2 §3 proof depends on binary M. Threshold: 0. UMP SATISFIED: O (DRP-2 §3 text) is a published document fixed independently of P (mechanism independence claim). M (step-by-step audit) finite deterministic. C6 SATISFIED: binary-dependent step found → FALSIFIED.

claim_registry.sample_falsification_observation: A step in DRP-2 §3 reading 'Since M has exactly two outputs, the probability table has exactly two rows, allowing us to compute I directly.' If such a step existed, the proof would require binary M as a necessary premise.

claim_registry.label: Exhaustive coverage
claim_registry.statement: The M-class taxonomy (C-DRP4.3) is exhaustive: every finite deterministic function from an observation class to a verdict space falls into exactly one of the four sub-classes. The full conservation law (C-DRP4.5) holds for each sub-class individually and therefore for all M.

WHERE.n_uncovered: integer, dimensionless. Count of finite deterministic measurement functions M satisfying condition (b) of T(P,O,M) that do not fall into any of the four sub-classes defined in C-DRP4.3 (binary, n-ary, continuous, probabilistic).

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: 1. Enumerate known M functions used in published falsification predicates across the corpus. 2. For each: classify into C-DRP4.3 sub-class. 3. Attempt to construct an M satisfying condition (b) that does not fit any sub-class. 4. If such an M is found: n_uncovered += 1. Output: n_uncovered. Finite procedure.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-DRP4.7 · Type F · Exhaustive coverage. Tests whether the M-class taxonomy is complete. Threshold: 0. A single uncovered M would require extending the taxonomy and re-proving the conservation law for the new class. UMP SATISFIED: O (M functions and their output spaces) determined by examining predicate structure, upstream of P (taxonomy completeness claim). M (classification procedure) finite deterministic. C6 SATISFIED: uncovered M found → FALSIFIED.

claim_registry.sample_falsification_observation: An M function whose output space is a non-Hausdorff topological space not embeddable in ℝ or [0,1] and not a finite discrete set. Such an M would satisfy condition (b) (finite deterministic) but fall outside the taxonomy.

placeholder_register.description: Outbound pass-forward: C-DRP4.5 (Full Conservation Law) → DRP-5. DRP-5 receives as Type A axiom and proves universality + auxiliary hypothesis protocol. ARCH-2 placeholder resolved when DRP-4 reaches PHASE-READY.
placeholder_register.target_claim: Full Conservation Law (Type A axiom in DRP-5)

pass_forward_register.label: Full Conservation Law: I(M(O);P) = 0 for all terminating M when O = f(P). DRP-5 receives as Type A and proves universality.

steps.description: Received inputs: C-DRP4.1 (A) from DRP-2, C-DRP4.2 (A) from DRP-3
M-class taxonomy: partition all terminating M by output type
Generalised epistemic information: extend I measure to all M-classes
Mechanism independence: DRP-2 §3 proof audited — no binary-specific steps
Full conservation law: I=0 generalised from binary to all M via structural property O=f(P)
Exhaustive coverage: conservation law verified for each sub-class individually

derivation_table_reference.principle_10_compliance: All constants and conditions trace to live edges (DRP-2 C-DRP2.2, DRP-3 C-DRP3.5) or IR axioms. No orphaned constants.

principle_10_audit.constant_or_condition: O = f(P) (observation class derived from proposition)
principle_10_audit.derivation_path: Received via C-DRP4.1 from DRP-2 C-DRP2.2 (live edge). The condition O=f(P) is the antecedent of the conservation law proved in DRP-2 §3. Not stipulated — derived from the UMP violation analysis in DRP-2.

principle_10_audit.constant_or_condition: I(M(O); P) = H(P) − H(P|M(O)) (generalised epistemic information)
principle_10_audit.derivation_path: Definitional extension of DRP-2 §3 information measure (C-DRP4.1, live edge) to all M-classes. Standard Shannon/differential entropy. No new empirical constants introduced.

Exhaustive classification of finite deterministic function output spaces by topological structure. Derived from condition (b) of T(P,O,M) via C-DRP4.2 (live edge from DRP-3 C-DRP3.5). Standard mathematical classification — no stipulated constants.
Function composition axiom (IR-DRP4-3). If O = f(P) and M is deterministic, then M(O) = M(f(P)) = h(P) is deterministic in P. Standard mathematical property — no empirical constants.

principle_10_compliant_note: All 4 audit entries have derivation_path values anchored at live edges (DRP-2 C-DRP2.2, DRP-3 C-DRP3.5) or IR axioms (IR-DRP4-3). No free constants or stipulated conditions.

phase_ready.note: CBT I-9 all 7 steps PASS — Session S56. Phases 0-3 complete (S56, combined build record). Phase 4 (AI layer) complete S56. Phase 5 (CBT I-9) complete S56. 1 C4-tracking placeholder (PH-DRP4.1: C-DRP4.5 → DRP-5), non-blocking. All 3 Type F claims have full 5-part predicates, vacuity witnesses, UMP compliance checks. All inbound edges LIVE. Principle 10 audit: 4 entries, all anchored at live edges or IR axioms. Pass-forward: C-DRP4.5 → DRP-5 LIVE. Registry update: 175 + 7 = 182 claims, 18 papers.

cbt_steps.step_3_derivation: PASS — 6-step derivation table D4-2.0. All steps IR-cited. No circular steps. 0 invalid steps. All 7 claims appear as derivation outputs.
cbt_steps.step_4_principle_10: PASS — 4/4 audit entries have derivation_path anchored at live edges or IR axioms. No gap_ids remaining. principle_10_compliant=true.
cbt_steps.step_5_dependency: PASS — C4 mode PHASE-READY-TRACKING. 1 C4-tracking placeholder (PH-DRP4.1), non-blocking. Pass-forward C-DRP4.5→DRP-5 registered LIVE.
cbt_steps.step_5b_where_scan: PASS — All WHERE field external references (T(P,O,M), DRP-2 §3, C-DRP4.3) have corresponding inbound edges, internal claims, or are evaluation procedure references.
cbt_steps.step_6_cross_corpus: PASS — No unregistered cross-corpus references. DRP-5 pass-forward registered.
